sanskrit:
न हि देहभृता शक्यं त्यक्तुं कर्माण्यशेषतः।
यस्तु कर्मफलत्यागी स त्यागीत्यभिधीयते॥
transliteration:
na hi deha-bhṛtā śakyaṃ tyaktuṃ karmāṇy aśeṣataḥ |
yas tu karma-phala-tyāgī sa tyāgīty abhidhīyate ||
literal_meaning: Never indeed for the embodied is it possible to abandon actions entirely; whoever is a renouncer of the fruits of action—he is said to be the true renouncer.
contextual_meaning: The mind cannot stop generating impulses to act; genuine renunciation is psychological surrender of outcome, not physical inaction.
psychological_state: Permission to remain active while relinquishing obsessive control over results; reduces performance anxiety and moral perfectionism.
trigger_scenario: A project manager on sick leave who keeps checking emails, realizing that delegating and trusting the team without micromanaging is the healthier 'renunciation.'
anchor_text: Keep doing your duty, but let go of craving for the outcome.
